## CSS Loader for Millennium v1.2.0

This release separates the Millennium companion from the desktop application
and changes the Windows backend to a pre-extracted onedir installation.

### Packaging and startup

- Replaced the compressed single-file backend with a pre-extracted onedir
  backend bundled inside the desktop installation.
- Disabled UPX compression to reduce antivirus false positives.
- The backend now runs directly from the desktop application's installation
  directory.
- Backend login startup now uses the current user's Windows Run registry entry
  instead of copying an executable into the Startup folder.
- Legacy Startup-folder backend copies are removed automatically.
- Added updated MSI installer artwork.

### Separate Millennium companion

The Millennium companion is no longer bundled with or installed by the desktop
application. Install it separately from the
[CSS Loader Companion repository](https://github.com/DevsNate/CSSLoader-Companion-Millennium).

Separating the companion allows the Millennium plugin and desktop application
to be installed and updated independently.

### Fixes and improvements

- Added a longer timeout for theme downloads and installations.
- Improved Store success, failure, and refresh handling so installation errors
  are reported consistently.
- Existing themes, profiles, and settings remain preserved during upgrades.

### Included artifact

- Windows 10 or 11 x64 MSI installer containing the desktop manager and onedir
  backend.

Unsigned applications can still trigger reputation warnings, but the new
packaging avoids the previous compressed single-executable backend layout.
